review_text: Quiet park location. We stayed on the water front side. Great view and a cooling breeze. Great spot, we will be back for a longer stay soon enough.
Great looking caravan park excellent van sites to choose from  location is great right on the water   cabin look neat and tidy grounds and van sites are well maintained.

Nice caravan park
Very quiet
Beautiful little park

We stayed on a ensuite site for 14 days (no school hols or public hols) thier website stated stay 4 pay for 3, we were charged $76 per night i asked about the deal stay 4 pay for 3 and was told that the deal is included in the price, I queried the price of $76 p/n and asked what would be the price without the deal and was told $90 per night 😲 we pay $100 p/n over Christmas at Fingal Bay. In the 14 days we weren't asked if we would like our ensuite cleaned which other parks we have stayed at offer cleaning, so we have come to Fanninghams Island leisure Park Anna bay across the water from koala shores, we are paying $35 p/n for a powered site (no ensuite) and the park is great, beautiful bush setting lovely pool, nice camp kitchen and we aren't camped on top of each other, so maybe have a look at fanninghams Island leisure Park before booking into koala shores, it's a lot cheaper and we think it's nicer relaxed Park.

This was our second stay at Koala Shores. We first stayed four years ago and the cabins were in pretty poor condition, which made us hesitant to return. However, because we were travelling with a group of friends, we decided to give it another go. We booked eight months in advance and were allocated a five-bedroom cabin (Cabin 23).

When we arrived, we were instead given Cabin 22. At first this didn’t seem like a big issue, until we realised it only had four beds. We were told nothing could be done that night and ended up sleeping on couches that weren’t sofa beds, with instructions to come back to reception the next morning.

The following day, staff first tried to move us into another cabin belonging to someone else in our group, which was completely inappropriate. Only after we pushed back were we told that the guests in Cabin 23 were checking out and that we could finally move into the correct cabin.

The cleanliness of the cabins was also very disappointing. There was dust and crumbs everywhere, including under the couches. Cutlery, pots and utensils still had food on them. If you stay here, be prepared to clean the bathrooms and floors yourself and bring your own kitchenware.

Things got even worse on our second-last night when the door lock stopped working and we were locked out. My husband spent a lot of time trying to fix it himself. Maintenance had no clue how to fix the lock, he watched on whilst my husband removed the lock, a part was needed, and then left us without a working lock for the remaining two nights. Despite calling the emergency line several times and being told someone would come back, no one ever did. If my husband wasn’t a tradesman, this situation would have been far more serious.

Dealing with all of this over a long weekend with three young children was incredibly stressful and completely unacceptable.

Also worth mentioning — the internet is terrible. Neither 5G nor the park’s Wi-Fi works properly, so if you need to get any work done, don’t count on having internet access.

If we could give this place zero stars, we would. We do not recommend staying here and will never be returning.

This is genuinely sad for me to write, but after this stay I feel I need to be honest.

I have been a loyal customer of this park for years, staying at least three times a year, and in the last two years I have also brought multiple groups with me. I’ve always loved staying here — until this visit.

I know exactly which cabin I had booked because I pre-booked it in person on my last stay, with a group of four other couples. While two of those couples later cancelled (which is understandable), I specifically asked to make sure we were still placed next to the other family who was staying in their caravan. Despite this, when we arrived with our five children, we were placed in Cabin 8 — a cabin we did not book — and were positioned on opposite ends of the park from the family we had planned to stay next to.

Cabin 8 was tiny, dirty, the beds were extremely uncomfortable, and the fans were so loud they banged nonstop all night. None of us slept. On top of that, there was a large group booking (around 30 families) nearby, with people up past 1am walking around, gathering outside our windows, talking loudly and making noise throughout the night.

The next morning I went to reception, explained the situation, and said I was disappointed that we were not given the cabin we had booked. I was told the only option was to pay $385 extra to upgrade — which I did — but only for two nights. This meant that during our five-night stay, we had to move three times.

The upgraded cabin was much more comfortable and is actually the type of cabin we regularly stay in. However, on the third day we were forced to move again into another small cabin.

Even during the “better” nights, we still didn’t sleep. At 3am there were kids knocking on our windows, groups gathering outside, loud talking, and small children banging on our doors — waking our younger kids.

When I approached reception again, I was told they were aware that this group was noisy, and that the guests had a culture of sleeping during the day and being active throughout the night. Despite this, no action was taken and no effort was made to manage the noise or protect other families’ rest. At no point was anyone apologetic — not for the wrong cabin booking, not for the constant disruption, not for us being moved three times in five nights, and not for our children being repeatedly woken during the night.

We were never offered any flexibility, extra time to pack and move, or any form of compensation despite paying over $2,680 for this stay, staying in three different cabins, dealing with constant disruption, rude staff, and rundown, uncomfortable cabins.

As a long-term loyal customer who has brought business and group bookings to this park, I feel extremely disappointed, disrespected, and let down. This experience has completely changed how I view this place amd I feel like I need to find another caravan park to give our loyalty to.

Very disappointed given the price we are paying for our site. Glad we arrived a day late so only have to be here 2 nights.  A good number of very messy and rundown looking permanent resident caravans and buses around, long grass and random things lying around looking like a dumping ground. When paying 110 a night to be next to that mess, its just not acceptable. ||No issue with permanents but there should be a standard level of neatness.||Plus side is nice pool, but that's all I can say

We stayed in the Koala House with our family of 7 over the Christmas week. We also had a service dog. The staff couldn't do enough to make our stay absolutely wonderful. The beds were so comfy, everyone commented on them. The pool and spa were very clean and obviously looked after. Great back yard for dog and kids to run around. Great entertaining area. If you want a 6 bedroom house (3 with ensuites) for a family get together, then you wont go wrong with this house. Staff were so helpful, especially Jo, who couldn't help us enough! What a great employee for Aspen. I give 110 out of 100!!!! Thank you Aspen.

My wife and myself really enjoyed the stay in our motorhome, right on the waterfront site. It was as relaxing and had a great water view which was excellent. The pool and facilities were good except for the lack of BBQ,s around the park which would be a long wait when the park is busy We walked around the foreshore track which was good. Just down the street was Tanilba Bay which was beautiful.
We would definitely return for future holidays. 👍

The worst caravan park i have stayed in  for over 10 years. 20 people booked in a group in advance. We had to put up with a construction crew for 2 days who were moving gravel fixing a pool that did not work and fixing  pool fences. I picked up 3 ten inch long steel stakes and gave to manager and was told that they were from work done on the sites we were camping on. Great for tyres. They over billed some of our group and it has taken over 10 days to get a refund .  They are also keeping peoples credit card details which i think is not coirrect without consent .but the worst thing overall is fir me to stand by managers work vehicle and listen to him describe our group on two way radio as winging old moles. This description is for a group of women from 69 to 80 years old. He also did this in front of one if his cleaning staff .  Iverall a realky bad news place on so mant kevels
Had great camping here

The amenities could be improved, particularly when it comes to cleanliness. At first glance the cabin appears “clean,” but it quickly becomes clear that the standard may rely heavily on guests tidying up before departure, with staff mainly replenishing linens and toiletries. The space feels a bit tired and doesn’t smell fresh, which could be easily addressed with more thorough cleaning. The floors were dusty, corners had built-up crumbs, and the shower had some grime.||The overall feel of the place is quite mediocre, and it wouldn’t take much to elevate it. Simple improvements—like more thorough cleaning, properly installed blinds for privacy, and updated TVs that are securely mounted—would make a noticeable difference.| I’d recommend bringing your own cleaning supplies and giving the place a quick clean so you’re not dealing with leftover dirt from previous guests.

Main pain points: Cleanliness, privacy.
"Superior" Cabin #22
The amenities could be improved, particularly when it comes to cleanliness. At first glance the cabin appears “clean,” but it quickly becomes clear that the standard may rely heavily on guests tidying up before departure, with staff mainly replenishing linen and toiletries. The overall space feels a bit tired and doesn’t smell fresh, which could be easily addressed with more thorough cleaning. The floors were dusty, corners had built-up dirt, and the shower had some grime. I’d recommend bringing your own cleaning supplies and giving the place a quick clean so you’re not dealing with leftover dirt from previous guests.

The overall feel of the place is quite mediocre, and it wouldn’t take much to elevate it. Simple improvements—like more thorough cleaning, properly installed blinds for privacy, and updated TVs that are securely mounted—would make a noticeable difference.

We loved this park. We were in prime location on a waterfront caravan site. This park has a waterfront location, boat ramp, great jumping pillow, plenty for the kids, nice open spaces, wildlife and shops nearby. We will definitely be back.

A chocolate wrapper was under the lounge left from previous holiday goers. Dirt on top of range hood and looks like its not part of a regular clean.  The fridge is missing two shelves and the veg crisper is also missing. The tv in the bedroom room worked on only a few stations. The kids beds were very very hard. My son has been sleeping on the lounge. The washing machine stopped working on day two. Staff offered to take our washing and do it for us and gave us new linen. Getting a new one tomorrow. The receptionist did give the kids a free paddle board hire to make up for the pool being unavailable. She also explained the work for the pool is 3 weeks delayed due to the weather. I told her it also happened 6 years ago when we came here. The pool then was also unavailable. We thought we would give the place a second chance. Staff were lovely and friendly. We are dissapointed. We only get holidays once a year and we dont get to choose when. I think it should be made mandutory to let occupants know when main features such as pools are unavailable. As this is meant to be a key feature on the ammenities. People choose holidays for convenience of having activities available for their families.
The best part is nature. The wild ducks come to visit. The bird life is great too. We saw kookaburras and rainbow lorikeets. Wild rabbits. We have not yet seen koalas. I went and feed cooked rice to the chickens. Its a great place for nature lovers.

A bit noisy and the pool wasn't open as they were repairing it (we didn't get any prior warning about this) but overall it was in a lovely location, close to a lake which was perfect for swimming. It is close to a number of attractions as well as shops and amenities.
Alice quite park with clean villa,s
The location is good.Our room is in front of the river with a nice and opening view, The room are clean and basic amenities are available, Travel to nearby supermarket is easy.

It was great staying here for my sisters birthday.
Staff were great letting us have a party.
Thanks for the stay and will be back soon.
Had my 50th birthday here. Robyn was amazing and made a stress free process. Extremely accomodating  and went over the top to help in every way. Thank you to all staff.
Excellent service at the front desk, reception staff were wonderful. Cabins were nice and clean aswell. We loved our stay in lemon tree we will be back next holidays!
Great park with lots to do with the kids. Loving the renovations. Located in a beautiful majestic location.

I'm so sorry but I'm going to be brutal on my review. I was in unit 19, top location near water, lots of activities for the kids to do, but the cleaning of the unit let's the park down. Never cleaned the windows and tracking wardrobe tracking the bathroom is atrocious.  These are little things that could be cleaned once a week. Maybe if you gave your cleaners a little bit more time to clean a unit it would be a five star accommodation. The kids bed were so thin you can feel the wooden slats under the mattress. They need to be thicker.
Because it was raining and the wind was so strong you could smell the dust, I'm allergic to dust and an asthmatic  I woke up with a headache and feeling stuffy. The toaster was broken, you should have electrical goods tested every 3 months.
Untill you get a five-star rating. You have no right to charge people $50 for not washing up their dishes and $100 for not removing your rubbish, if you're not going to clean the units properly. Very disappointing. Buy a steam cleaner, this is good for The bathroom and kitchen use it around the taps on the toilet seat on the rim of the toilet in the window tracks, floors any marks on the walls you will save on chemicals and time .
Also we had a blackout at about 9pm, no contact from the park. The power was restored sometimes in the early hours of the morning. Thank God for mobile phones otherwise you would be in compete darkness. No emergency lights in units. And service, what service. No wifi that reaches my unit.

This facility is amazing!! wonderful staff, clean and tidy cabins, great outlook and surrounding park area. Very comfortable bed and a great shower.|Heaps of amusement for the kids|Thoroughly recommend, 10 out of 10 from me.|I will be back!!
Hired am Ensuite site for 4 nights. Was a gamechanger with two small grandchildren.|Lovely location and plenty to do or do nothing and relax.
The cabin was very comfortable and had everything you need. The staff are very friendly. I recommend to stay at Koala Shores any time

Beautiful ❤️ location birds and animals beautiful! Ppl very friendly! Loved the pooled out of our kitchen window, and lake front door! Loved the little verandah were U go and sit look at lake and pool and relax and have Ciggie and have beer how wonderful 👍 even had Bbq one nite that was great!
But needed coat hungers, and more towel rails to hung towels!
And few more cushions! And pillows!
Otherwise I love my birthday 🎈🎈 few days there 2 days it ,
it!☀️☀️👍👍❤️❤️.

We had a fantastic stay at Koala Shores over the June Long Weekend. The ability to have fires (contained) and our dog made it an easy choice for us. We were on site 6 - along the waterfront, near the boat ramp. It was a truly stunning spot. Inclusion of a coffee van was a nice inclusion on the cold mornings. Kids had a great time! |Note - Site 6 is a LONG way from the amenities - great spot if your self sufficient, bit of a trek if in a tent or camper. Would definitely recommend.

We have stayed in our can at Koala Shores twice so far. The first time I extended and I have booked again. I have to say this is one of our favorite parks to state at. We love to fish so it is convenient. The staff are awesome the management are incredible and go out of their way to assist. Nothing is an issue. We go in off season and the park is beautiful and quiet the amenities spotless. I highly recommend this park.
They may gave upgraded because of rain I'm not sure but it was a fabulous cabin everything you could want. Very happy and would definitely go back

Big Park. Lots of sites. Fair price. Beautiful spot for water sports. Lots of activities to do for kids. Lovely pool. No shops close by if you don't bring a car. Sites fairly close together. Friendly staff. Great location and peaceful. Nice bush walk track.
5th time at this beautiful place.

Paid $1500 for a few nights in a cabin
No wifi
No mobile reception
No tv signal

No answers from staff to fix issues after following up several time
Great park. Cabin had all that I needed.
Gr8 place to holiday here been here several times and going again next month.

Really nice service but the rooms were dated and smelly. The bathroom doors wouldn’t close properly which was problematic to have a private shower. The tv reception was bad and no wifi or phone reception in the rooms. We left after one night.
Gr8 place for camping, pity the weather is a bit wet at time, but still loving it here.

Stayed here 2 nights, toilet building was not very clean. Loads of noise going on long after 10 pm.

And to top it all off, my stuff got stolen from the camp kitchen fridge.

We love this place as it is a nice and quite caravan park that is well maintained daily with plenty of stuff for the kids to do there . When you get there and see the lake you just want to jump in and go for a swim .
The area was nice, but the fire works going off at 9:20pm and then again at 10:20pm was distressing and frustrating. Especially since the loud noise cut off is at 10pm.
Have had a great few days here at Koala Shores, cabin was clean and comfortable, had everything that we wanted, comfortable bed and pet friendly! Highly recommended and we'll be back.

The location is amazing, located on the waters edge, the sun sets are amazing. The amenities are always clean and staff make sure the noise is kept down after hours. There is lots of wildlife to see and close to the shops.

Stayed at koala shores last year with my family had a great time. Staff were very friendly and helpful and made us feel very welcome. We stayed in an ensuite site which was quite dated but we recently stayed there again and the ensuites had been renovated which made it so much more enjoyable. Hats off to the lovely staff lady on the phone which helped me book this spot. It was like staying in a 5 star hotel with a short trip to the bathroom. You don’t get quality like this at any other campsite . Would and will definitely stay here again

Great location. Camping and caravan sites are well organised and clean. Cabins are well appointed and furnished. Dogs allowed  is a great bonus. However, the quantity and variety of kitchen equipment and utensils could be improved. Staff is very helpful.

I recently had a week stay here with my family in the larger, more expensive cabin and was quiet disappointed.
The fridge had no door shelves, no crisper and was not cleaned well - very hard to store things in with missing pieces.
Dishwasher was unable to be used as the labels are all worn off you cannot see any setting options.
The outside railing down the stairs has frayed wire which resulted in 2 bad injuries to small kids - staff notified but never followed up with anything.
Bathroom tap had a continuous leak, may be small incontinences but when we one has received injury and the amount of money paid it was a bit of a let down.
Trip with our van, nice park. With wildlife in the backyard .
Perfect location beach and walks ways
Great cabins and a lovely water front location, pool was great for the kids, other facilities could do with an upgrade
Beautiful place to stay on the weeks with the Fam!
Perfect family holiday!
Most of the staff are helpfull great place
Always a great time with my family making memories at Koala Shores

Lovely and helpful staff. Cabin was good but  old, dusty and worn out lounges and rooms so if you are the allergic type bring your clarantyne or Dustbuster . A very enjoyable park. Most importantly the staff are wonderful and great location. W

Beautiful place, very clean.
Our only issue was the exit gate would be better to be automatic to let you out.

Maintenance is starting the lapse. The kids zone was all but closed with the jumping pillow locked up and the putt putt golf course and tennis court badly worn and cracked. The amenities van site we had was in bad shape, but the location is great. A bit of TLC and this park would be great. Won't be coming again until it's fixed up

Another great spot for camping with all the facilities you need! It’s perfect for caravans or cabin stays, with plenty of space for tents—though we were the only ones camping during our visit. While the camp sites are close to each other, the beautiful location, stunning views, and abundant wildlife—birds, rabbits, and possums—make up for it. The office team was incredibly humble and helpful, even offered us an en-suite site option. It’s not secluded but ideal if you enjoy having others around, and access to swimming, a communal kitchen, and nearby cafes and restaurants. Highly recommended!

Biggest disappointment was the toilets, if you don't have a cabin there are only 2 toilets in the whole place far walk from camp normally has a line up to wait.... definitely should invest into more however enough negative putt putt was fun camping pillow was empty most the time also offers ping pong tennis and basketball so great spot if you have kids not far from pub just upgrade the toilets !!!! Put in a toilet block

I have very bad experience with of them, Barbecue  was not clean, when we went bed we found bed linens has sand and mark. Called them to chane but they only gave me bed linen.
Next day morning sink and toilet was block
Great little spot

Have a powered caravan site which was unlevel in both directions. Hitch was so low that I couldn’t get my jockey wheel in and had to use my jack to level it off. Otherwise nice park , hot showers and clean. Didn’t see a single koala though
Great place  to visit.  Clean and well maintained amenities and grounds. Well worth the visit.
Great location for powered sites.

Highly Recommend!
We booked a standard room and was upgraded before our arrival as the room wasn’t up to standards. Which is great that they take notice and pride in the cleanliness of their accommodation. We as a family of 4 enjoyed our stay,Things to do for the kids and plenty of space to fish. Very family friendly,Staff are polite  and the whole environment is great. Will definitely be visiting again.
Visited here for a Wedding so it was a nice place to rest with out breaking the bank
Tranquil setting overseen by friendly staff

Stayed on a powered site with concrete slab, some sites have green matting instead. Ensuite site’s available. The amenity’s were clean and in good condition. Nice pool. Good camp kitchen. Has its own boat ramp and small jetty. Not much room for check in. Surrounded by nice trees my site backed onto trees and ferns and was lovely. Will stay again.
Stayed in the koala house and the beds are comfortable and wifi is fast speed
Stunning caravan park

Clean and tidy units
Comfortable beds
Nice pool for kids and adults
Fabric on outdoor furniture was stained. Reported to office. They apologized and promised to attend.
Walk to river/harbour shore
Lovely clean park. Dog friendly in certain areas. Awesome view of the inlet. Boat ramp As well.
Had an ensuite  site down near the water it was beautiful clean and quite park
A very pretty park. Not too big. Some spots are right on the water.
Beautiful water views and great van park

Is a great little spot near nelson bay area for a budget, BUT if you book at counter ensure they quote right price, was quoted and paid for what they said and after we left they charged more as they to my card because of their mistake and I had to dispute with bank to get money back. Make sure to document everything
Only reason i was able to getmy funds back. I won't be back
Great place to stay. Staff are friendly too.

Caution Advised with Koala Shores' Rescheduling/Cancellation Policy.

Despite providing over four weeks' notice, my attempt to reschedule our booking was met with an inflexible response.

It's important to note that I didn't request a refund, just a credit to use at a later date. However, I was informed that due to the nature of our online rate, neither refunds nor credit options were available.  On the rate booked, the website states "No cancellations or refunds are allowed" but never mentions rescheduling or credits.

This policy seems overly stringent, especially considering the ample notice given, which would likely allow for the resale of the site we had booked.

From a customer experience perspective, this has been a disappointing experience and could have been easily avoided.

Potential guests should be aware of these strict policies before booking directly with Koala Shores.

The location was lovely and the pool was very nice, unfortunately it was closed on the day of our arrival for emergency maintenance. The cabin we stayed in (supposedly superior) was in dire need of a proper clean and some serious money needs to be spent to even bring the standard up to an average level. There were numerous spiders hanging from the ceilings, ants coming in through the skirting board, a number of light globes were missing, tiles were cracked in the bathroom, the vanity was rotting due to water damage and the exhaust fan was absolutely ancient - I have never seen anything like it! The TV had no standard channels (only very limited viewing) and the wi fi was hit and miss.  It was incredibly overpriced for what is on offer. We definitely would not stay there again.
A bit older but well maintained and clean , great facilities and staff.

We stayed in cabin 25 which is a premium priced cabin
The floorboards in kitchen are rotted
You nearly go through floor when you. Step on them
Very dangerous
Great place great for children an plenty of activities

We booked 2 camp sites for 1 night the service was fantastic from start to finish nothing was a problem lovely staff to deal with gounds & amenities good good camp kitchen great pool & all set on a awesome location great place to stay for the whole family
Just visiting friends,  never stayed, not a very busy park when I was there, but close to the water and quiet.
Toilets and Amenities block were way too far away from the power sites near the water -Absolutely ridiculous to be at the top of the park and not much lighting of a night to walk there .
Great location and kids had a ball only down side was the cabin we stayed is in need of a good spring clean, not sure the beds or couch have been cleaned under in a while.
Really loved our stay there in the caravan. Lovely pool very relaxing
Was a fantastic weekend, we love coming here all the time. The staff are so helpful and friendly.
Cabin is very clean, bed is very comfortable.  Overall stay is amazing!
Loved our time here great service 👍
On the water beach area lovely place
We had a great time at this park the amenities were old but kept  very clean and working. The walk on the shoreline was very enjoyable  . We will be back thank you.
Very relaxing and beautiful water views from the site I was on.

Only visited the rel's.
It looked neat clean and well kept.
Great for children

Site 28, nice site along the tree line. Great for kids to run around with a bit more space. Lovely pool and proximity to the inlet, perfect for little kids, inflatables, kayaks and the like.
Amenities are fine, normal park like.
Kids park not great at all. Jumping pillow is good. Rubbish point is out the front so a bit of a trek but fine.
Great park ,helpful friendly staff 👍we enjoyed our stay, keep an eye out for the koalas 🐨 💕

Lovely peaceful spot to spend a few days.
No recyclable bin
Great van sites

We loved staying in this beautiful park, the sites are large, the facilities are clean and in good condition.  There is lots of wildlife with regular visits from the Kookaburras and the water views.
The pool is more than your ordinary rectangle one. There are two play areas for kids. The very friendly and helpful team is working to continually improve and update the park.
There is a board walk along the shoreline to the town. Courtesy buses are available to the clubs for a meal and drink at reasonable prices.
We are looking forward to returning sometime in the future.
